title: Constant LHP-eigenvalue count across the family (connectedness / no-spillover step)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Absence of imaginary-axis eigenvalues alone does NOT give Hurwitz stability of the whole family — one also needs the number of open-left-half-plane eigenvalues to be constant over $\mathcal A$.

**To prove:** Since $\mathcal A$ is path-connected and convex, and (by the eigenvector/monotonicity lemma) no member has an imaginary-axis eigenvalue when the four Kharitonov vertices are stable, the count $\#\{\lambda_i(A(p)):\mathrm{Re}\,\lambda_i<0\}$ is locally constant (eigenvalues cannot cross $\mathrm i\mathbb R$) hence globally constant $=n$ (its value at a stable vertex). Therefore every $A(p)$ has all $n$ eigenvalues in the open LHP, i.e. is Hurwitz.

**What must be supplied:** rigorous statement that LHP-count is locally constant away from imaginary crossings (continuity of eigenvalues + they cannot jump across $\mathrm i\mathbb R$ without crossing it), and that connectedness propagates the vertex value to all of $\mathcal A$. Argue without invoking the argument principle / Hermite–Biehler if the no-characteristic-polynomial constraint is read strictly; the spectral-continuity formulation suffices.
